body: ## Purpose Up to now, our fluxnet sites have been using UTC offsets that are internally consistent but not following the standard convention. This PR fixes their signs and updates how they're used to remain consistent. Also, the timezone for MOz was wrong and is now fixed. Closes #1343 and #1301 ## Content - [x] fix timezone/UTC offset for MOz fluxnet site - previously we had 7 (mountain standard time) but this should be -6 (central standard time, no daylight savings) - [x] fix sign of UTC offsets - for North American sites these should be negative following the standard convention - [x] verified plots look good - non-Ozark fluxnet sites are unchanged. For Ozark, both the simulation output and data curves shifted by 1 hour ## Example of Ozark plot changes before this PR: after this PR: ---- - [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: follow UTC offset sign convention; fix Ozark timezone

## Purpose 
- Add error metrics to the tutorials since we include these tutorials in the paper.
- Since the freezing front was changed to save at every timestep, the indexing was incorrect to extract the solution at 12h, 24h, 50h. This also fixes that.
